content:
Profile Name: Rampelli Sai Eshwar
Role: AI Systems Engineer (LLMs, Computer Vision, Distributed Systems)
Location: Hyderabad, India
Summary: AI/ML Engineer specializing in LLM-driven systems, multi-agent orchestration, and real-time computer vision pipelines. Experienced in building production-grade AI applications including requirement-generation agents, video intelligence systems, and distributed AI architectures with strong focus on scalability, reliability, and optimization.

Current Status (What he is doing right now / currently working on): Right now, Sai Eshwar is studying Minor in Artificial Intelligence at Indian Institute of Technology (IIT), Ropar. He is currently working on the following active projects: Barney, TelePort. He is available for AI Engineering roles.

Experience at 0101 Digitall
Role: AI/ML Engineer Intern
Duration: Dec 2024 – Mar 2025
Work done:
Built a Requirements Agent using LangChain that converts ambiguous client ideas into structured software requirements (user stories, acceptance criteria).
Developed a real-time horse movement analysis system using computer vision with visual overlays for training feedback.
Optimized deep learning models for real-time inference, improving latency and stability in video processing pipelines.

Education: Minor in Artificial Intelligence at Indian Institute of Technology (IIT), Ropar (Jan 2025 – Jan 2026)
Details:
Focused on AI systems, machine learning theory, and applied deep learning.
Worked on real-world AI projects involving computer vision and intelligent systems.

Education: Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech) at Kamala Institute of Technology and Science (Aug 2020 – Jul 2024)
Details:
Major in Computer Science and Engineering.
Built foundational knowledge in data structures, algorithms, and system design.

Technical Skills:
AI/ML: Deep Learning (CNNs, Transfer Learning, Fine-tuning, Quantization), Agent-based Systems (LangChain, Multi-Agent Orchestration), Prompt Engineering, Model Optimization
Computer Vision: Object Detection (YOLO, SSD), Real-time Video Processing, OpenCV, MediaPipe
Tools: PyTorch, TensorFlow, FastAPI, Django, LangGraph, FFmpeg, Docker, Redis, PostgreSQL, Vector Databases
Programming: Python, SQL

Project: Barney (Status: Under Progress - Currently working on this now)
Type: Multi-Agent AI Orchestration System
Problem: Single-LLM systems struggle with complex multi-step tasks due to lack of planning, memory, and tool integration.
Solution: Built a modular multi-agent system that decomposes tasks, assigns them to specialized agents, and executes them using tools and memory-aware reasoning.
Technologies: LangGraph, Python, FastAPI, Redis, PostgreSQL, Vector Databases, Docker

Project: Gravity Edits (Status: Completed)
Type: AI Video Editing System
Problem: Manual video editing is slow and requires expertise.
Solution: Built a dual-agent pipeline (Inspector + Director) to convert natural language instructions into executable video editing workflows.
Technologies: Gemini 2.5 Pro, FastAPI, FFmpeg

Project: VirBoard (Status: Completed)
Type: Real-Time Computer Vision Interaction System
Problem: Traditional input devices limit natural interaction.
Solution: Gesture-controlled virtual drawing system using real-time hand tracking.
Technologies: OpenCV, MediaPipe, Python

Project: VidLingo (Status: Completed)
Type: Speech Recognition + Translation System
Problem: Manual subtitle creation is slow and not scalable.
Solution: Automated pipeline for subtitle generation, translation, and synchronization.
Technologies: Google Speech-to-Text, Translation APIs

Project: TelePort (Status: Under Progress - Currently working on this now)
Type: Android TV Aggregation Platform
Problem: Fragmented OTT ecosystem reduces content discoverability.
Solution: Unified Android TV launcher with deep-link optimization.
Technologies: Android TV, Deep Linking
